what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that points to a board item via a board:// URI.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
id follows the att-* naming pattern
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference an external board item
The file is purely a reference stub with no payload or explanatory text
concerns: No author or editor is recorded
No title, description, or payload explains what is attached
The referenced board item cannot be resolved from this file alone
notes: A bare attachment ledger stub linking att-8b27f13d to board item item-7ad77ae2 in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
What is the nature of board://item-7ad77ae2?
Who created this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated; the file actually functions as a timestamped reference record tying an attachment id to a board item id.
intention_signals: It appears designed to persist a discoverable, time-stamped relationship between a channel attachment and a board item so the link can be retrieved and audited later.
novelty: Use of a board:// URI scheme to reference a board item from a channel attachment record.
contribution: Provides a persistent, timestamped reference that links an attachment identifier to a board item identifier, supporting traceability and cross-referencing in a channel memory system.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata record (att-8b27f13d) in the dragnet-development channel that references board item item-7ad77ae2 via a board:// URI and records a creation timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To log and link a channel attachment to a specific board item for later retrieval, correlation, and audit.
